question: For the `Book` class (from Q1), overload the stream insertion operator (`<<`) to enable printing `Book` objects directly to `std::cout` in a formatted way (e.g., 'Title: ... Author: ... ISBN: ...').

test_cases.input: Book("The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy", "Douglas Adams", "978-0345391803")
test_cases.expected: Title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy Author: Douglas Adams ISBN: 978-0345391803
Title: To Kill a Mockingbird Author: Harper Lee ISBN: 978-0061120084

errors: The stream insertion operator uses `std::endl` which flushes the stream buffer unnecessarily, impacting efficiency. It also uses multiple `+` operators for string concatenation, which creates several temporary `std::string` objects, leading to less efficient code compared to chaining `<<` operations directly on the output stream. While functionally correct, these practices are suboptimal.
